motion: Merge PR #32 after session 3 fixes (commit d72decb). Audit F1 seed auto-apply, F2 filter lower-bound, CD-1 backup safety.

findings.description: CD-1 regression: the shouldBackup guard compares backup===dst, which fails the orphaned-backup+evolved-config path. If Run #1 teardown crashes then user edits seeki.toml, Run #2 preserves the stale backup and teardown silently overwrites the user's edits.
findings.fix_description: Replace backup-vs-dst comparison with dst-vs-configContent: if DST !== configContent, DST is user config and must be backed up; if DST === configContent, prior run left test config — preserve existing backup. configContent is already in scope at L86.

findings.description: Justfile test-e2e recipe omits destructive-operation warning. d72decb added TRUNCATE + INSERT on every run; Justfile:33 invocation gives no signal before data destruction. Before d72decb the suite was read-only.
findings.fix_description: Add comment warning that test-e2e truncates vehicle_logs and soc_readings; recommend SEEKI_SKIP_SEED=1 for non-dedicated DBs.
findings.consensus: severity disputed by ADVOCATE-1; arbiter ruled as condition

non_blocker_followups.description: Credential guard at global-setup.ts:171 omits !pass check — unset SEEKI_TEST_DB_PASSWORD produces generic psql auth error instead of specific 'password not set' message
Filter upper-bound uses toBeLessThanOrEqual(initialTotal) — with seed data the reduction is strict; toBeLessThan(initialTotal) would give a tighter assertion

resolved_in_session: F1 partial-seed risk (Playwright aborts on globalSetup failure — tests never run against empty DB)
CD-1 inverted-logic framing (scenarios 1-3 correct; only orphaned-backup path regresses)
O-4 loud failure under SEEKI_SKIP_SEED=1 is correct behavior
O-2 teardown-without-DB-cleanup is conventional for dedicated test DBs
O-3 empty-password guard — imprecise diagnostic, not silent failure

prior_session_status.session_3: 3 findings + 1 Critical Discovery, all applied in d72decb (verified intact; S4-1 is a regression in the CD-1 fix itself, not in other session-3 fixes)

summary: Session 4 auditing d72decb surfaced two findings: S4-1 (merge blocker, unanimous) is a regression in the CD-1 fix — the shouldBackup guard fails on the orphaned-backup+evolved-config path, destroying user-edited configs. S4-2 (arbiter-ruled condition) is a documentation gap: Justfile introduces a new destructive operation without warning. Both are addressable in the same PR. Core F1/F2/seed idempotency fixes verified correct. No Critical Discoveries.
